rubric_en: Resistance to tech is brewing among digital natives
rubric_zh: 数字原住民中对科技的抵触情绪正在酝酿

paragraphs.en: “We will free the iPad babies,” went one chant at the Summer of Ludd. “No Gemini, no GPT, no Grok, no Claude,” went another. The festival for big-tech sceptics, held in New York from June 28th to July 5th, stayed mostly true to its principles. Phones were banned. The event had no social-media presence. Organisers relied on posters and word of mouth to publicise the festival. Perhaps as a result, the crowds were small. Yet those who came, most of them in their 20s, reflect an ambivalence about technology that is increasingly common among Generation Z.
paragraphs.zh: 在“Ludd之夏”期间，有人高喊：“我们要解放iPad婴儿。”另有人喊道：“不需要Gemini，不需要GPT，不需要Grok，不需要Claude。”这场在纽约于2026年6月28日至7月5日举行的反对大科技活动节，基本遵循了自己的原则：禁止携带手机。活动没有社交媒体账号。组织者主要靠海报和口碑来宣传这场节日。也许正因为如此，现场人群规模不大。然而前来参加的人多数在20多岁之间，他们对科技的态度，正反映出在Z世代中越来越常见的矛盾情绪。

paragraphs.en: Members of this cohort are the first to have grown up entirely in the age of the smartphone. Now many are reflecting on what it has cost them. In 2024 nearly a quarter of Gen Z-ers told Harris, a pollster, that they wished smartphones had never been invented. More recent polling by Pew found that over half support banning under-16s from social media. Many are trying to reduce their own screen time, deleting apps or setting limits. Others, like the self-described Luddites in Manhattan, go further. At one point during the festival, devices were “executed” after standing trial.
paragraphs.zh: 这一代人是第一批从小就完整成长在智能手机时代的人。如今，许多人开始反思它给自己带来了什么代价。2024年，几乎四分之一的Z世代受访者告诉民调机构Harris，自己希望智能手机从未被发明。皮尤研究中心近期的另一项民调显示，超过一半的人支持禁止16岁以下青少年使用社交媒体。许多人正试图减少自己的屏幕时间，删除应用程序或设定使用上限。也有人像曼哈顿那些自称“卢德分子”的人那样更进一步。在节日的某个环节，设备还“被审判后处决”。

paragraphs.en: The original Luddites were 19th-century textile workers who resented the mechanisation of their industry. They were not opposed to all technology, but feared for their livelihoods. Today’s young Luddites do not shun the digital world entirely, but object to tracking tools, invasive algorithms and devices’ effect on their social lives. Artificial intelligence has added another fear: 70% of Gen Z think AI will have a negative impact on jobs, according to Marist.
paragraphs.zh: 最初的卢德分子是19世纪的纺织工人，他们反对行业机械化，并非反对所有技术，而是担忧失去生计。今天的年轻卢德分子并没有完全远离数字世界，却反对追踪工具、侵扰性算法，以及设备对他们社交生活的影响。人工智能又带来了另一重担忧：据Marist研究机构，70%的Z世代认为AI将对就业产生负面影响。

paragraphs.en: Some Gen Z-ers are trying to lead more analogue lives, swapping screen time for offline pursuits. Sales of flip phones and cassette tapes have risen. New “Luddite clubs” encourage people to give up their smartphones and social media, at least for a while. Ironically, going offline is being popularised online. Influencers post about gardening and puzzles as antidotes to doomscrolling, reframing tactile activities as a “luxury” lifestyle. A video on TikTok, encouraging a switch to an old iPod, has 3m views. (One commenter asks how the device works without streaming.) Pinterest, a social network, is courting users by promising “it’s about life, not likes”.
paragraphs.zh: 一些Z世代正在尝试过更“类模拟”的生活，用线下活动取代屏幕时间。翻盖手机和卡带式录音机的销量都在上升。新出现的“卢德俱乐部”鼓励人们暂时放下智能手机和社交媒体。具有讽刺意味的是，离线生活正在通过线上被“传播”起来。网红们发布与园艺和拼图相关内容，作为对无意识刷屏的解毒方式，并把手工活动重新包装成一种“高端”生活方式。TikTok上一则提倡换用旧款iPod的视频获得了300万次观看。（有评论者问：没有流媒体，这台设备怎么用？）社交平台Pinterest也在吸引用户，宣传语是“强调生活，而非点赞”。

paragraphs.en: Even the Summer of Ludd is not entirely free from the tensions of the modern world. One attendee admits she was her group’s “designated smartphone user”, helping everyone else navigate the city. Still, the Luddites believe in their cause. Since giving up his smartphone three years ago, Nick Plante, 25, reports milestones such as being able to finish a book again. “This is the best I’ve felt, probably ever in my life.” ■
paragraphs.zh: 即使“卢德热潮”也并未完全摆脱现代世界的紧张与矛盾。有人坦言她曾是小组里“指定的智能手机使用者”，负责带领大家在城市里导航。尽管如此，卢德派依然坚定地相信自己的主张。尼克·普兰特（Nick Plante）25岁，表示自从三年前放下智能手机后，他达成了许多里程碑，比如再次能够完整读完一本书。“这是我一生中可能体验过的最好状态。” ■

paragraphs.en: Stay on top of American politics with The US in brief, our daily newsletter with fast analysis of the most important political news, and Checks and Balance, a weekly note that examines the state of American democracy and the issues that matter to voters.
paragraphs.zh: 通过《美国简报》掌握美国政治最新动向：这是一份每日通讯，快速解析最重要的政治新闻；《制衡》则是每周专栏，审视美国民主的现状以及选民关心的议题。
